description: A minimal CLI tool that scans SRE testing infrastructures for exposure to known third-party vendor data leaks (e.g., BrowserStack breaches) by checking credentials, monitoring leak databases, and alerting on compromised services.
core_features: Scans local config files and env vars for vendor credentials (BrowserStack AWS keys, API tokens)
Queries public breach databases for vendor incidents matching your email/domains
Generates risk reports with remediation steps for detected exposures
CLI alerts for HIGH severity leaks affecting testing infrastructure
mvp_scope: 3-day build: 1) CLI scanner for BrowserStack-specific leaks (AWS keys, emails from 2014 breach patterns), 2) Check against HaveIBeenPwned API for user emails, 3) Simple terminal report with PASS/FAIL/WARN status, 4) Config file support (~200 LOC). No DB, no scheduling.
success_metric: Detects 100% of planted BrowserStack AWS credentials in test configs AND flags emails from known breach datasets within 5s execution time (measured via pytest).
source_problem: Critical Security Breaches from Vendor Data Leaks
